Source_Species.reason: PhPreHB is described as a precursor peptide gene from Pseudostellaria heterophylla.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper consistently studies orbitide genes in Pseudostellaria heterophylla.

Applied_Species.reason: PhPreHB is from P. heterophylla and was transiently expressed in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both Latin names appear in the paper: Pseudostellaria heterophylla and Nicotiana benthamiana.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PhPreHB is explicitly treated as a precursor peptide gene for orbitide biosynthesis, i.e. a RiPP/orbitide precursor.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state module 1 precursor gene PhPreHB is necessary for the pathway and co-expression with PhPCY3 produced HB in N. benthamiana.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: PhPreHB is the precursor for heterophyllin B (HB) in the Results.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: HB is presented as an orbitide/cyclic peptide.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The abstract/background says orbitides from P. heterophylla have potential for improving memory and treating diabetes, but this paper did not directly test HB bioactivity here.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: HB was detected and quantified as highest in roots.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: The paper states PhPreHB was among precursor genes mined from transcriptomes, and Supplemental Figure 10A showed PhPreHB downregulated in PhPCY3-silenced leaves.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Transient expression in N. benthamiana with PhPCY3 produced HB.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: PhPreHB was tested by transient co-expression/LC-MS, and PhPreHB transcript plus HB were examined in the PhPCY3 VIGS context.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point matches Results: precursor role for HB, HB production with PhPCY3, and reduced PhPreHB transcript in PhPCY3-silenced leaves with reduced HB.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper states precursor peptides have conserved leader/follower regions with diverse core peptides, and that short CDS precursor genes were mined from transcriptomes.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The paper specifies use of the pEAQ-HT vector, but does not state 'promoter not specified' as a result field; vector support is clear, promoter identity is not given here.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Functional interaction with PhPCY3 is directly tested by co-expression producing HB.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper presents precursor genes and the transient platform as useful for biosynthesis/discovery of orbitides, which supports engineering value.

Omics_Data.reason: Precursor genes were mined from transcriptome data, and RNA-seq raw reads were uploaded under SRA accession SUB14839290.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: PhPreHB was studied under Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana and in the context of PhPCY3 VIGS in P. heterophylla.

Source_Species.reason: PhPrePE is a precursor peptide gene from Pseudostellaria heterophylla.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source species throughout the study is Pseudostellaria heterophylla.

Applied_Species.reason: PhPrePE is from P. heterophylla and was functionally tested in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both Latin names are explicitly used in the paper.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PhPrePE is treated as an orbitide precursor peptide gene.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state PhPCY3 cyclized PrePE to PE, showing precursor substrate function.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The product is explicitly pseudostellarin E (PE).

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: PE is one of the orbitides/cyclic peptides analyzed in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: PE content was significantly higher in roots than other tissues.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: Supplemental Figure 10A is described as showing PhPrePE downregulated in leaves when PhPCY3 was silenced.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: PhPCY3 cyclized PrePE to PE in the transient N. benthamiana system.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Supported by transient co-expression with PhPCY3, LC-MS detection, and VIGS-associated expression/metabolite analysis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points are described in the Results for PrePE/PE.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Use of pEAQ-HT vector is explicit, but no promoter is specified.

Omics_Data.reason: Precursor genes were mined from transcriptome data; RNA-seq accession SUB14839290 is provided.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Transient expression in N. benthamiana and VIGS in P. heterophylla are both described.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Direct functional relationship with PhPCY3 is shown by cyclization of PrePE to PE.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The study frames precursor genes and PhPCY3 as tools for synthetic production of orbitides including PE.

Source_Species.reason: PhPrePF is a precursor peptide gene from Pseudostellaria heterophylla.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source species is Pseudostellaria heterophylla.

Applied_Species.reason: PhPrePF was examined via transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana, with source species P. heterophylla.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both species names appear explicitly in the study.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PhPrePF is part of the precursor peptide genes for orbitides.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Supplemental Figure 7 is described as showing PhPCY3 cyclized PrePF to PF.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: PF is an orbitide/cyclic peptide in the paper.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: PF is stated to be present in roots in Figure 1C text, but Figure 1D only explicitly says HB and PE/PG contents were significantly higher in roots; support for PF root accumulation is less explicit in quantified Results.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: PhPCY3 cyclized PrePF to PF in transient expression assays.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Transient co-expression with PhPCY3 and LC-MS are explicitly described for PrePF/PF.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Both points are consistent with the Results statement that PhPCY3 cyclized PrePF to PF.

Applied_Promoters.reason: pEAQ-HT vector is explicit, but no promoter is specified.

Omics_Data.reason: Precursor genes were mined from transcriptome datasets and RNA-seq accession SUB14839290 is given.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: The relevant treatment is Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression in N. benthamiana.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Functional interaction with PhPCY3 is directly shown by product formation.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The platform is presented as enabling biosynthesis of known orbitides, which supports synthetic production utility for PF.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The source species is explicitly Pseudostellaria heterophylla.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results state PhPCY3 cyclized PrePG to PG.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: PG content was significantly higher in roots than other tissues.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Transient assays showed PrePG plus PhPCY3 yielded PG.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: Supported by transient co-expression, LC-MS, and VIGS-associated expression/metabolite analysis.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three statements match the Results for PrePG/PG.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The pEAQ-HT vector is given, but promoter identity is not specified.

Omics_Data.reason: Transcriptome mining and RNA-seq accession SUB14839290 are reported.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Both transient expression and VIGS contexts are explicitly described.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: Direct functional interaction with PhPCY3 is shown in co-expression assays.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The transient platform is used for synthetic production/discovery of orbitides, supporting application value for PG biosynthesis.

Source_Species.reason: PhPCY3 is a gene from Pseudostellaria heterophylla.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The species studied is explicitly Pseudostellaria heterophylla.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: Zheseng No.2 was the cultivar used for tissue/orbitide/RNA-seq analyses and Kangbing No.1 seedlings were used for VIGS, but PhPCY3 itself is not explicitly assigned as sourced from both varieties.

Applied_Species.reason: PhPCY3 was tested in P. heterophylla and in Nicotiana benthamiana.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Both Latin names explicitly appear in the paper.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: Zheseng No.2 and Kangbing No.1 are plant materials used in different experiments, but 'NA' for N. benthamiana variety is not a paper-stated value.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PhPCY3 clusters with SvPCY1, is discussed as an S9 serine protease/PCY1-like enzyme, and Figure 5 identifies the prolyl oligopeptidase domain PF00326.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper presents PhPCY3 as a peptide cyclase/PCY1-like key tailoring enzyme.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: PhPCY3 is repeatedly placed in the orbitide biosynthesis pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: Results show PhPCY3 mediates macrocyclization/cyclization of Caryophyllaceae-type cyclic peptides (orbitides).

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: PhPCY3 performs the cyclization step generating the final cyclic products, i.e. the terminal macrocyclization step.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper directly tests HB [14-35] and states PhPCY3 cyclizes PrePE/PrePF/PrePG; inability to use HB [14-21] directly supports dependence on longer precursor context including follower peptide.

Primary_Product.reason: Results support production of HB, PE, PF, PG, and release of HB [22-35] as byproduct in vitro.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states PhPCY3 excises the C-terminal region/follower peptide and cyclizes the propeptide/core peptide into orbitides.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: All listed known and four novel orbitides are reported as produced using the PhPCY3 platform.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These products are orbitides/cyclic peptides, as stated throughout the study.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper provides background claims that orbitides/HB have potential memory, antidiabetic, and anticancer-related activities, but these functions were not experimentally tested for PhPCY3-derived products in this study.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Roots are clearly supported, but 'especially tuberous roots' is not established for all listed PhPCY3 products in Results; known orbitides were quantified in roots generally.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Transient expression, in vitro assays, and VIGS all show PhPCY3 is essential/primary for orbitide cyclization and accumulation of HB, PE, and PG.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: PhPCY3 directly forms the cyclic end products from precursor peptides.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: PhPCY3 was highly expressed in roots in Figure 2A.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: In infiltrated N. benthamiana leaves, co-expression with precursor genes produced orbitides.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper explicitly calls PhPCY3 the key tailoring gene and states it is the primary enzyme for orbitide biosynthesis / dominates orbitide cyclization.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: All listed contexts—root tissue expression/accumulation, transient expression, and VIGS—are explicitly described.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are directly used in the study to validate PhPCY3.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each numbered statement is directly supported by Results and figure descriptions.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion states PhPCY3 is the second validated orbitide cyclase after SvPCY1; Figure 5 gives S571/D662/H704 and N500/S502; inability to use HB [14-21] is directly shown.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The paper explicitly frames PhPCY3 and the platform as useful for biosynthesis/discovery of known and novel orbitides in synthetic biology.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Use of pEAQ-HT vector is explicit, but promoter identity is not specified in the paper text provided.

Omics_Data.reason: The study used transcriptome and genome mining, and RNA-seq raw reads were deposited under SUB14839290.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: PhPCY3 functionally acts on precursor peptides PhPreHB/PE/PF/PG, but these are precursor proteins/substrates rather than clearly demonstrated protein-protein interaction partners.
